executive_summary.description: A high-level overview of the findings, summarizing the primary methods for generating PDFs from Safari on macOS, their trade-offs, and the key challenges involved in automation.

recommended_headless_solution.description: Provides a specific, actionable recommendation for the most robust and reliable method for programmatic, headless PDF generation from web content on macOS, based on the research findings.

script_example.description: An exact example of the AppleScript and System Events UI scripting sequence required.

parameter_control_limitations.description: Details on the inability to programmatically set margins, scale, paper size, or orientation directly via AppleScript.

dialog_suppression_possible.description: Indicates whether the print dialog can be suppressed (it cannot).

required_permissions.description: A summary of the macOS permissions (Accessibility, Automation) needed for the script to run.

reliability_issues.description: Common reliability problems such as dependency on UI element names, system language (localization), and timing.

parameter_list.description: A detailed list of all available parameters for the page.pdf() method, including their purpose and data types.

css_handling_details.description: Explanation of how Playwright handles print-specific CSS, including @media print, @page rules, and page-break properties.

header_footer_template_support.description: Information on the capabilities and limitations of the headerTemplate and footerTemplate options.

output_quality.description: Characteristics of the generated PDF, such as text selectability, font embedding, and vector graphic rendering.

render_wait_behavior.description: Describes how Playwright waits (or doesn't wait) for page content to fully render before generating the PDF.

playwright_internal_implementation.description: An analysis of how Playwright's `page.pdf()` is implemented internally. Details its reliance on the Chrome DevTools Protocol (`Page.printToPDF`) for Chromium and the corresponding lack of full support in WebKit and Firefox backends.

webkit_safari_print_rendering_details.description: In-depth analysis of how Safari's WebKit engine renders pages for PDF output. Covers its support for `@media print` and `@page` CSS rules, and its specific, often problematic, handling of elements with `position: fixed`, `position: sticky`, and `overflow: hidden` properties.

safari_vs_chrome_rendering_comparison.description: A direct comparison highlighting the known differences, bugs, and rendering inconsistencies between Safari/WebKit and Chrome/Chromium when generating PDFs, particularly concerning layout, fonts, and CSS support.

method_name.description: The name of the alternative method (e.g., WKWebView createPDF, CoreGraphics, wkhtmltopdf).

api_and_usage.description: Description of the API or command-line usage for the method.

maintenance_status.description: The current maintenance status of the tool or library (e.g., actively maintained, archived).

output_quality_summary.description: A summary of the output quality, including text selectability, vector fidelity, and potential artifacts.

automation_suitability.description: An assessment of how suitable the method is for headless, programmatic automation in a service or daemon.

alternative_pdf_generation_methods.description: A list of alternative approaches for programmatic PDF generation on macOS. Each item will be detailed to cover its API, maintenance status, output quality, and suitability for automation. Includes methods like WKWebView's `createPDF()`, CoreGraphics, wkhtmltopdf, and screencapture.

cups_based_alternatives.description: An exploration of using macOS's underlying printing system (CUPS) for headless PDF creation. Covers the use of `cupsfilter`, `lpr`, and virtual PDF printers, including setup, quality trade-offs, and security constraints.

header_and_footer_techniques.description: A comparative look at generating custom headers and footers. Contrasts Safari's lack of native support with Playwright's `headerTemplate`/`footerTemplate` feature and the use of CSS `@page` margin boxes as a potential cross-browser solution.

edge_case_handling_strategies.description: Discusses strategies and limitations when dealing with complex PDF generation scenarios, including multi-page documents, very long or infinite-scroll pages, and the rendering of SVG and Canvas elements.

password_protection_method.description: Details on the tools (e.g., qpdf, Ghostscript) and steps required to add password protection and encryption to a PDF.

pdf_a_compliance_method.description: Details on the tools (e.g., Ghostscript) and validation utilities (e.g., veraPDF) used to convert and verify PDF/A compliance.

post_processing_requirement.description: Indicates that these features are not native to browser PDF generation and require a separate post-processing step.

font_embedding_considerations.description: Analysis of how web fonts are handled during PDF generation across different methods, including strategies for ensuring fonts are properly embedded to avoid missing characters or incorrect rendering in the final document.

required_permissions.description: A list of specific macOS permissions required for automation, such as Accessibility and Automation (TCC).

sandboxing_restrictions.description: Notes on the limitations imposed by the macOS app sandbox on automation scripts.

ui_scripting_reliability_issues.description: Common causes of failure in UI scripting, including localization, timing issues, and UI layout changes.

mitigation_strategies.description: Recommended strategies to improve the reliability of UI automation scripts.

comparative_analysis_of_methods.description: A summary comparison of the different PDF generation methods based on key criteria such as output quality (text selectability, vector fidelity), performance, automation suitability, and maintenance status of the required tools.
